message:
Update Deacon patrol formula to use bd wisp gc command (gt-psj76.2)

The session-gc step was referencing non-existent `gt gc --wisps` command.
Updated to use the correct `bd --no-daemon wisp gc` command with examples.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -36,7 +36,7 @@
       "id": "session-gc",
       "title": "Clean dead sessions",
       "needs": ["orphan-check"],
-      "description": "Clean dead sessions.

Garbage collect terminated sessions:
- Remove stale polecat directories
- Clean up wisp session artifacts
- Prune old logs and temp files
- Archive completed molecule state

```bash
gt gc --sessions
gt gc --wisps --age=1h
```

Preserve audit trail. Only clean sessions confirmed dead."
+      "description": "Clean dead sessions.

Garbage collect terminated sessions:
- Remove stale polecat directories
- Clean up orphaned wisps
- Prune old logs and temp files
- Archive completed molecule state

```bash
# Clean wisps older than 1 hour (default threshold)
bd --no-daemon wisp gc

# Or with custom age threshold
bd --no-daemon wisp gc --age 2h

# Preview what would be cleaned
bd --no-daemon wisp gc --dry-run
```

The `--no-daemon` flag is required when the bd daemon is running, since wisp gc needs direct database access.

Preserve audit trail. Only clean sessions confirmed dead."
     },
     {
       "id": "context-check",
